dialogue.text:
Title: “The Adventures of Gloopy and Glitter”

Production company: Inovism Productions (UK) for Kids WB (USA) and S4C (UK)

Networks: S4C and Pop (UK) Kids WB (USA)

Episode: “The Great Space Entry”

Scene 1: Gloopy and Glitter’s Home Planet

- The scene opens on a vibrant alien world filled with goo and glimmer.
- Gloopy, the laid-back teal blob with a charming smile, is listening to spaced-out tunes with headphones that wrap around his entire gelatinous body.
- Glitter, a sparkling fuchsia blob that leaves a trail of glitter in her wake, is concocting a shimmery potion in their blobby lab.

Glitter: “Gloopy, check out my new Sparkle-Boost Serum! It’ll make our shimmer shine across the galaxy!”

Gloopy (pulling off headphones): “Groovy, Glitter! But remember the last time? We ended up as intergalactic disco balls!”

Glitter laughs, her sparkle intensifying.

Scene 2: The Space Entry Competition

- The scene transitions to a colorful and dazzling space arena, buzzing with aliens of all shapes and sizes.
- A banner reads, “The Great Space Entry – Dazzle the Judges for a Galactic Prize!”

Gloopy: “I hear this year’s prize is a warp drive that could power our blob-mobile for light years!”

Glitter: “Ooh, Gloopy, let’s win this! I’ve got just the thing!”

Scene 3: Preparation

- Gloopy and Glitter are backstage, prepping for their entry.
- Gloopy stretches into funny shapes while Glitter applies her Sparkle-Boost Serum.

Glitter: “Time to shine!” (She dabs the serum on both, causing them to glitter spectacularly.)

Gloopy: “We’ll be the brightest blobs this side of the Milky Way!”

Scene 4: The Performance

- The competition begins, and various alien acts display their talents.
- Gloopy and Glitter slide onto the stage, leaving a shimmering trail.

Announcer: “Here come Gloopy and Glitter, the Glowing Wonders of Gloopiter!”

- The duo performs a shining acrobatic routine, bouncing and blending into dazzling shapes, radiating light that forms constellations.
- The audience is awestruck as Gloopy and Glitter create a nebula of vibrant colors, culminating in a grand finale that illuminates the entire arena.

Scene 5: Victory and Surprise

- Applause erupts. Gloopy and Glitter are handed the warp drive.
- As they celebrate, Gloopy nudges Glitter.

Gloopy: “Hey, Glitter, check it out!”

- He points to a pair of starry-eyed alien kids who are playing, imitating Gloopy and Glitter’s shimmering forms with wonder and delight.

Glitter (teary-eyed): “We’re not just shining stars, Gloopy. We’re role models!”

Gloopy: “Bright blobs for a bright future, Glitter!”

Scene 6: A Sparkling Conclusion

- The episode closes with Gloopy and Glitter driving off in their now warp-drive-powered blob-mobile.
- They zipline through space, leaving a shimmery streak across the stars.

Voiceover: “And so, Gloopy and Glitter zoom off to new adventures, spreading their sparkle far and wide, promising to return with more tales from the cosmos!”

- The screen fades to black as we hear the echoing giggles of Gloopy and Glitter, with the twinkle of interstellar glitter in the background.

Title: “The Adventures of Gloopy and Glitter”

Episode: “A Touch of Slime”

Scene 1: The Wondrous World of Wobbleonia

- As the sun rises over the slime-covered mountains of Wobbleonia, we see Gloopy and Glitter in their blob-mobile traversing the starlit cosmos.
- Suddenly, the blob-mobile jolts, causing them to make an unexpected landing on a planet blanketed in shades of pink.

Gloopy: “Whoa, Glitter! This place is like a cotton candy dream!”

Glitter: “Look at the shimmering pink sands, Gloopy. It’s so… sparkly!”

Scene 2: The Pink Encounter

- While exploring, they meet a friendly pink alien named Blibby, who sports big curious eyes and a gentle smile.
- Blibby is fascinated by Gloopy and Glitter’s shiny appearance and is eager to make new friends.

Blibby: “Greetings! I am Blibby. This world is called Rosalux. I’ve never seen creatures like you before!”

Glitter: “We’re the gleaming wanderers from Gloopiter! I’m Glitter, and this is Gloopy. Our ship had a bit of a hiccough.”

Scene 3: The Curious Touch

- Blibby is both awed and intrigued by Gloopy and Glitter’s shimmering skin.
- With a polite request, Blibby reaches out to touch Gloopy, wondering if the shine is a solid shell.

Blibby (with wonder in voice): “May I? Your glow is so intriguing!”

Gloopy: “Sure thing, Blibby. But prepare for a slime surprise!”

- When Blibby touches Gloopy, her hand comes away covered in teal goo, much to her amazement.

Blibby: “Oh! It’s soft and squishy! And… it’s not sticky! What a marvelous sensation!”

Scene 4: The Slime Session

- Gloopy laughs and jiggles, while Glitter uses her sparkles to clean up the goo from Blibby’s hand, leaving a twinkle on her skin.
- Overcoming the initial shock, Blibby is now brimming with excitement and requests a lesson in slime play from Gloopy and Glitter.

Glitter: “Let’s give Blibby the ultimate slime experience! Ready to get your hands goopy?”

Blibby: “Yes, please! Teach me your slimy ways!”

Scene 5: Slimey Shenanigans

- Gloopy shows Blibby how to bounce and mold the slime into different shapes, sparking joy and laughter.
- Glitter teaches Blibby how to make sparkly goo, combining their textures into a dazzling visual display.

Blibby (with glee): “This is incredible! Our people love new forms of art. With your slime, the possibilities are endless!”

Gloopy: “Sharing our slime is our pleasure, Blibby!”

Scene 6: An Artsy Farewell

- As the day ends, they invite Blibby to share this newfound art with the rest of Rosalux.
- The pink world soon becomes a canvas of colorful slime sculptures and glittery designs, thanks to their collaborative effort.

Glitter: “You’re a natural, Blibby! Rosalux looks even more stunning now!”

Blibby: “Thank you, Gloopy and Glitter. We’ll treasure this slimy, sparkly art forever!”

Scene 7: Departure to Dazzle Another Day

- Gloopy and Glitter bid farewell to their new friend and her pink planet as they board their repaired blob-mobile.
- They set off into the cosmos, eager for the next whimsical adventure, leaving behind a legacy of laughter and slime-art on Rosalux.

Voiceover: “And so, Gloopy and Glitter continue their journey across the stars, spreading creativity and joy in the slimiest of ways!”

- As the blob-mobile zooms away, the camera pans to show the glowing pink planet, now adorned with slime sculptures shimmering under the starlight.
Write character descriptions about the 2, their appearance, personality and abilitieas
Character: Gloopy

Appearance:
- Gloopy is a laid-back teal blob with a semi-translucent body that exudes a gentle glow.
- His eyes are two small floating orbs within his gelatinous form, exuding warmth and friendliness with every blink.
- A charming smile is often etched near what would conventionally be the front of his blobby body.
- Whenever Gloopy moves, his body wobbles and reshapes, showing his flexible and adaptable nature visually.

Personality:
- The essence of chill, Gloopy is the kind of blob that takes life at a relaxed pace, believing in going with the flow.
- He’s got a sunny disposition, is never in a hurry, and loves to soak up the cosmic tunes with his entire being.
- A great listener, Gloopy is supportive and often serves as the voice of reason in his adventures with Glitter.
- His open-mindedness and creativity make him a hit in the artsy circles of the galaxy and an endearing figure to friends.

Abilities:
- Gloopy’s malleable form allows him to stretch, squish, and reshape himself into a variety of configurations, from thin pancake-like extensions to bouncy spheres.
- He can absorb certain impacts like a jelly, which also helps him get out of sticky situations, both figuratively and literally.
- Gloopy has a unique talent for creating and controlling sound waves with his slimy vibrations, using them to communicate or to soothe friends with his space-themed tunes.

Character: Glitter

Appearance:
- Glitter is a sparkling fuchsia blob with a brighter and more solid appearance than Gloopy, emitting a constant stream of fine glitter that leaves trails wherever she goes.
- Like Gloopy, her eyes are floating sparkly specks that always seem to be twinkling with excitement.
- Her smile gleams brightly, often punctuated by bursts of shimmer when she’s particularly joyful or enthusiastic.

Personality:
- Full of vim and vigor, Glitter is the dynamo of the duo, always eager to embark on new adventures and concoct new experiments in their bubbling lab.
- A fountain of ideas and initiatives, she’s the driving force behind their escapades, often sparking action with her sparkly personality.
- Resourceful and inventive, Glitter often combines her love for all things shiny with practical solutions, though her enthusiasm can occasionally lead to dazzlingly unpredictable consequences.

Abilities:
- Glitter’s body is infused with a mysterious sparkle dust, which she can control and manipulate to create awe-inspiring light shows or to enhance their visibility in various performances.
- She has a unique ability to concoct various potions and serums, many of which have glittery effects that can be both beneficial and mesmerizing.
- Her sparkles have a subtle, magical quality, capable of cleaning, repairing, or creating things, making her a handy blob to have around in a crisis or for a touch of pizzazz.

Together, Gloopy and Glitter are an unstoppable team, complementing each other’s abilities and personalities as they embark on intergalactic adventures, spreading good vibes and glitter dust across the cosmos.

Title: “The Adventures of Gloopy and Glitter”

Episode: “The Barren Planet of Shadows”

Scene 1: Lost in the Cosmos

- The blob-mobile zips across the cosmos, its warp drive allowing Gloopy and Glitter to push the boundaries of their explorations like never before.
- Suddenly, the warp drive sputters, and alarms blare. They’re pulled into the gravitational field of an uncharted planet.

Gloopy: “Whoa, the warp drive is acting up. We’re going in for an unexpected landing, Glitter!”

Glitter: “Hang on, Gloopy! Let’s make this an opportunity to discover something new!”

- The blob-mobile makes a bouncy touchdown onto the surface of a dusty, gray planet devoid of color and light.

Scene 2: The Mysterious Terrain

- Stepping out of their ship, Gloopy and Glitter look around at the stark landscape. Mountains loom in the distance, and the ground is littered with jagged rocks.
- The planet is in perpetual twilight, with a dim star casting long, creeping shadows.

Gloopy: “This place could use some of our sparkly charm, don’t you think?”

Glitter: “Definitely. It’s so… drab.”

Scene 3: The Shadow Stir

- As they traverse the dull terrain, seeking materials to repair the blob-mobile, Gloopy’s gooey footprints and Glitter’s sparkly trail are the only sources of brightness in this gloomy world.
- Unexpectedly, the shadows cast by the dim star begin to move and swirl around their bright paths. It’s as if the shadows are alive, curious, and drawn to their light.

Glitter (puzzlement shimmering in her eyes): “Do you see that, Gloopy? The shadows are moving!”

Gloopy: “They seem to like our glow. Hey there, shadows, we come in peace and sparkles!”

Scene 4: The Connection

- As they continue, Gloopy and Glitter start to play with the shadows, creating shapes with their bodies and watching the shadows mimic them.
- This interaction seems to delight the shadows, who begin to form patterns and symbols on the ground, signaling some form of communication.

Glitter (excitedly): “Gloopy, they’re smart! Let’s see if we can understand them.”

Scene 5: Learning and Teaching

- Gloopy and Glitter try different movements and gestures while observing the shadows’ responses.
- After some time, a rudimentary form of dialogue emerges—through the language of light and shadow, they begin to understand one another.

Gloopy (nodding thoughtfully): “They’re telling us they haven’t seen light like ours in ages. They’ve missed it.”

Glitter: “And they’re saying they want to help us fix the ship! How incredible!”

Scene 6: Cooperation

- With guidance from the shadows, Gloopy and Glitter locate the necessary materials scattered around the barren planet.
- The shadows playfully assist, enveloping objects in darkness to highlight them against the gray backdrop.

Gloopy: “Thanks, friends! This sure is teamwork of a whole new shade!”

Glitter: “Your turn to shine, my little shadows!”

Scene 7: The Transformation

- As the blob-mobile is being repaired, Glitter gets a bright idea. She gathers her potions and concocts a special mixture of her glitter and Gloopy’s goo.
- Spreading the mixture onto the landscape, a remarkable transformation occurs. The dull terrain begins to sparkle, and the shadows start to dance with color.

Glitter (with a heart full of glee): “A little bit of Gloopy, a little bit of Glitter, and voilà!”

Scene 8: A World Reborn

- The lifeless planet comes to life, bursting with colors as the shadows reflect the newfound light from every shimmering rock and sparkling crevice.
- The shadows, now intertwined with the light, create an ethereal glow that stretches as far as the eye can see.

Gloopy (in awe): “We didn’t just find a new world—we helped create one.”

Glitter: “And we’ve made new friends who taught us the beauty that lies in the shadows.”

Scene 9: Farewell and Forward

- With the blob-mobile repaired and the memories of a once barren planet now teeming with prismatic hues, Gloopy and Glitter bid their new friends goodbye.
- The shadows wave in their own fluid, graceful way as the duo blasts off, leaving behind a legacy of light in the darkness.

Voiceover: “And with their hearts a little fuller, Gloopy and Glitter continue their odyssey amongst the stars, forever seeking to brighten the cosmos, one rejuvenated world at a time.”

- The screen fades to black, with the echoes of Gloopy and Glitter’s laughter trailing off, the barren planet they left now aglow with vibrant shadows and radiant sparkles.

THE END

11:30 The Hollow. User1: Hey everyone! Did you all catch the launch of CW4Kids on the CW today? It was supposed to be this huge rebranding from Kids’ WB, but it all looked pretty much the same to me, just with a different logo. They even had the same schedule and promos.

User2: Yeah, I tuned in this morning expecting something totally different, but it was almost like nothing changed. My little brother didn’t even notice the switch to CW4Kids until I pointed out the new logo to him.

User3: I read that the real changes are coming in April, with a complete overhaul of the programming block. But starting January 20th, we’re beginning to see some of the 4Kids content making its debut. Seems like they’re starting slow and then hitting us with the big changes later.

AnimatedFan4Life: Kind of sad to see Kids’ WB go, but looking forward to seeing what 4Kids does with the block. “Pokemon Indigo League” and “Sonic X” are being added to the schedule later this month, and I can’t wait for those!

ToonWatcher: Have you all seen the future programming list? “Chaotic” and “TMNT: Back to the Sewers” sound interesting. I miss the old 4Kids block, so it’s kind of nostalgic to see them take over CW.

User1: Yeah, I noticed “Squish” and “The Amazing Adrenalini Brothers” got the axe for the new additions. I’m curious how the CW4Kids identity is going to evolve. Will it keep some of that old WB flavor, or are we looking at a total 4Kids takeover?

90sCartoonKid: I think CW4Kids should keep some of the legacy content while bringing in fresh shows. Kids’ WB had some great shows that shouldn’t be forgotten. Here’s hoping the overhaul in April will give us the best of both worlds.

MangaManiac: Just waiting for “Yu-Gi-Oh 5Ds” to hit the CW4Kids lineup. They teased future programming, and that’s what I’m most excited about. 4Kids always did well with Yu-Gi-Oh series.

DigiDestined: Kinda mixed feelings about the rebranding. Kids’ WB was iconic, but 4Kids had some solid shows too. The transition period seems like a smart move to not alienate the current viewers. Just hope the overhaul keeps the quality up.

SaturdayMorningFan: Noticed the bumpers still had that Kids’ WB vibe with just a logo swap. I’m intrigued by how they will rebrand the whole aesthetic come April. This gradual shift could be a good way to ease the audience into the changes.

User4: Does anyone know if the timeslots will change drastically? You know, with certain 4Kids shows having particular requirements and all that?

TVTimeTraveller: No significant timeslot changes announced yet aside from the two show swaps on Jan 20th. We might see a shuffle in April, but hopefully, they’ll keep the fan-favorites in prime positions.

User5: Honestly, as long as they keep airing “Batman: The Brave and the Bold” and “Teen Titans Go!”, my Saturdays are set. Though I am curious to see what new shows 4Kids will bring to the mix, just hope they don’t mess with a good thing!

CW4Kids
-----

CW4Kids is a children’s television programming block on The CW owned-and-operated stations. It is programmed by 4Kids Entertainment, which took over after the block’s rebrand from Kids’ WB on January 6, 2024. The block aims to deliver a mix of action, comedy, and adventure cartoons suitable for a young audience.

Owner: Warner Bros. Animation (transitional management under 4Kids Entertainment)

### Notes

- CW4Kids is a revamped version of the Kids’ WB block, maintaining some of the previous programming while introducing shows catered by 4Kids Entertainment.

- The CW4Kids block broadcasts every Saturday morning, providing multiple hours of animated programming to entertain and engage younger viewers.

- Programming on CW4Kids consists of both newly developed series and well-loved shows that have been reintroduced to capture both new and nostalgic audiences.

- The block includes a balance of genres, featuring predominantly action-oriented shows while dedicating slots to comedy-driven series like “GoGoRiki” and “The Adventures of Gloopy and Glitter” to maintain variety.

- Some of the former staples of Kids’ WB, such as “Teen Titans Go!” and “Scooby-Doo: Mystery Incorporated,” have been phased out as part of the transition to the CW4Kids brand and programming strategy under 4Kids Entertainment.

### Trivia

- “GoGoRiki,” initially taking the slot of “Teen Titans Go!,” is prepared for a later move into the slot previously occupied by “Johnny Test,” signaling an intention by the network to position it as one of the flagship comedy programs.

- The inclusion of classic shows like “Pokémon: Indigo League” and “Sonic X” is a nod towards the legacy of 4Kids Entertainment’s past programming blocks, aiming to bring in a sense of nostalgia for audiences familiar with these series.

- “The Adventures of Gloopy and Glitter” experienced a brief hiatus but was later brought back as a permanent part of the CW4Kids lineup, indicating the show’s popularity and demand for its return among the viewers.

Write a TVTropes Screwed By The Network example where (Most shows from Kids WB were screwed over by the blocks transition to The CW4Kids, Some shows like Scooby Doo: Mystery Incorporated and Ultimate Spider-Man were booted off after they had ran out of new episodes, while other like Squish, The Amazing Adrenalini Brothers and Teen Titans Go were kicked randomly, wheras Xiaolin Showdown and Johnny Test held on till the revamp in April, In the odd instance of The Adventures of Gloopy and Glitter, which was cancelled by Kids WB, 4Kids reversed the decision)
Screwed By The Network / Western Animation

The transition of Kids’ WB to The CW4Kids under the programming management of 4Kids Entertainment was a bumpy road for many fan-favorite shows. While some managed to secure their spot for a while, the destiny of many others was less fortunate, as they seemed to be screwed over by the network.

For starters, stalwarts like “Scooby-Doo: Mystery Incorporated” and “Ultimate Spider-Man” enjoyed their run until the well of new episodes dried up. They were then unceremoniously ditched from the lineup, leaving fans in the lurch without their beloved series.

On the flip side, hits like “Squish,” “The Amazing Adrenalini Brothers,” and “Teen Titans Go!” fell victim to random expulsion. Despite solid ratings and devoted fanbases, these series were unexpectedly axed with the network offering little to no explanation, leading to speculation about the reasons behind their sudden disappearance.

There were survivors of the initial culling, though their fate was only delayed. “Xiaolin Showdown” and “Johnny Test,” both anchor shows of the erstwhile block, clung on by their fingertips until the April revamp spelled the end of their tenure on The CW4Kids. It was a clear indicator that the new management was steering the block in a different direction, prioritizing their own branded content over the Kids’ WB legacy.

However, in a twist befitting the most intricate of animated plots, “The Adventures of Gloopy and Glitter” experienced a rollercoaster ride with the network. Originally axed by Kids’ WB, indicating a likely bleak future for the blobby duo, the new landlords at 4Kids stepped in and performed a surprise about-face. In a move that delighted fans, 4Kids reversed the cancellation, restoring “Gloopy and Glitter” to the lineup and crowning them as one of the block’s flagship shows.

The stark differences in these shows’ fates highlighted the uncertain world of network television programming where ratings, brand synergy, and management whims can make or break shows, often leaving both their characters and their fans on cliffhangers without resolution.
Write a Adored By The Network example saying that Code Lyoko was originally to be removed in April, but was kept, also talk about the flagship shows, and one is an example of Network To The Rescue
Adored By The Network / Western Animation

While many once-beloved series from the Kids’ WB days faced the chopping block during the transition to The CW4Kids, managed by 4Kids Entertainment, “Code Lyoko” emerged as a remarkable exception and stands as a textbook example of being adored by the network.

Initially, “Code Lyoko” seemed destined to share the same fate as its fellow shows, with an April removal marked on the network’s calendar. Fans prepared to bid farewell to the adventures of Jeremie, Aelita, and the rest of the Lyoko Warriors. However, as the dreaded month approached, it became apparent that the network had a change of heart. To the joy and surprise of its dedicated fan base, “Code Lyoko” remained steadfast in its programming slot even beyond the April revamp.

This decision to hold onto “Code Lyoko” indicated that the show held a special place in the network’s favor, perhaps due to its unique blend of 2D and 3D animation, its engaging storytelling, or its consistent performance with viewers. Ensuring the show’s continued presence amidst the sea of new and returning programs, it became clear that “Code Lyoko” was more than a show for The CW4Kids—it was a treasured jewel.

In discussing flagship shows adored by the network, “The Adventures of Gloopy and Glitter” is an exemplary illustration of the ‘Network to the Rescue’ trope in action. Although they were initially canceled by Kids’ WB, 4Kids Entertainment, recognizing the potential in the series and the love it received from the audience, took the extraordinary step to revive it. Not only was the show brought back to the airwaves, but it was also positioned as one of the block’s leading attractions.

“GoGoRiki,” too, enjoyed an elevated status as a network darling, quickly becoming a cornerstone of the comedy lineup. After taking over the slot from an established favorite (“Teen Titans Go!”), “GoGoRiki” further solidified its standing by later moving into the “Johnny Test” slot, indicating the network’s significant investment in the show’s broad appeal and success.

These instances show that while the whims and strategies of network executives can often lead to the untimely end of many fan-favorite shows, they can also serve as the saviors for some, providing them with unexpected lifelines and platforms for greater success. In the case of “Code Lyoko,” “The Adventures of Gloopy and Glitter,” and “GoGoRiki,” The CW4Kids indeed showed a level of adoration that would secure these series’ legacies for years to come.

4Kids Entertainment
November 28, 2023

The CW Television Network
3300 W Olive Ave,
Burbank, CA 91505

Attention: CW Programming Department

Re: Transition of Kids’ WB to CW4Kids - Incremental Changes and Introduction of New Lineup

Dear CW Executives,

We are reaching out to update you about the exciting changes planned for the transition of Kids’ WB into the new CW4Kids program block, which is set to commence following the turn of the year. Our team at 4Kids Entertainment has been diligently working to ensure a seamless transition that respects the legacy of Kids’ WB while introducing a refreshed and engaging lineup tailored to our young audience.

As we approach the December 31st timeline, we would like to confirm our plan to officially change the block name to CW4Kids. Alongside this rebranding, we are implementing incremental scheduling updates leading up to our major rebrand launch in mid-April. This will allow us to slowly introduce new series from the 4Kids catalog and to test audience reception to various content formats and arrangements.

During this transition, we will maintain the distinctive comic book-style bumpers and aesthetic developed by Kids’ WB, in an effort to preserve a sense of continuity and familiarity for the viewers. This visual style will be replaced with a fresh and contemporary look coinciding with the full spectrum launch of CW4Kids in mid-April.

The following flagship shows from 4Kids will spearhead our new programming direction:

- “GoGoRiki” – A delightful, light-hearted animated series that is set to become a cornerstone of our comedic lineup.
- “Yu-Gi-Oh! Arc-V” – The latest installment in the globally popular franchise, continuing the tradition of exciting duel-centric adventures.
- “Viva Piñata” – A vibrant animated series based on the popular video game that brings a fun-filled world to our viewers.

Additionally, we will carry over two shows from the existing Kids’ WB lineup all the way through to the April rebrand to maintain continuity for our audience. We have selected “Code Lyoko” and “Xiaolin Showdown” to be part of the adventure till the final rebrand, based on their long-standing popularity and the passionate fan base they have cultivated over years of broadcast.

Please rest assured that we are dedicated to honoring the concluding narratives of outgoing shows, such as “Scooby-Doo: Mystery Incorporated” and “Ultimate Spider-Man,” providing them with the proper finale as we phase out the current program slate.

As we embark on this journey, we are keen to receive ongoing feedback from all parties to ensure that our programming decisions are aligning with network expectations and audience trends.

We look forward to this enthralling new phase for the Saturday morning experience, and we are confident that CW4Kids will become a cherished weekend tradition for children across the country.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
Director of Programming Strategy
4Kids Entertainment

Also, here is the KWB/CW4K transition

Phase 1: December 2, Promos start airing announcing the change, saying that "From January 6, this kids network is changing it's name to the CW4Kids"

Phase 2: January 1, Website renamed to cw4kids.com

Phase 3: January 6, New name live

Phase 4: January 9, 4Kids begins to take some control from WB, they get the right to make schedules starting from January 27, 

Phase 5: January 13, Promos begin airing for Pokemon and Sonic X

Phase 6: January 27, Pokemon and Sonic X begin airing, replacing Squish and The Amazing Adrenalini Brothers, Also, promos for the debut of GoGoRiki begin, as well as the "Super Duper Late-Winter" lineup, which takes effect from Febuary 17

Phase 7:  January 29, The "Kids WB" division at Warner Bros Discovery is dissolved by 4Kids and WBD, Warner Bros Animation is now just a temporary shareholder, 4Kids now has full editorial control, although the block will still be played out from WB HQ untill the new Feb lineup starts

Phase 8: Febuary 14: A account and points system is added to cw4kids.com, games are also added

Phase 9: Febuary 17: The new lineup debuts, introducing Ultimate Muscle and Kirby: Right Back At Ya, Also, Gloopy and Glitter returns to the block, having last aired when it was Kids WB in late October, The block is now also played out from 4Kids

Phase 10: Throughout March, the VO on the promos will change to 4KidsTV announcers, Also, cw4kids.com will look more like 4kids.tv, in preperation for the full redirect beginning in late March, Warner Bros Animation loses all stakes in CW4Kids as well, and it becomes entirely 4Kids

Phase 11: Late March, cw4kids.com redirects to 4kids.tv, promos for the new look and final new shows begin

Phase 12:  April 13, The final changes are made, the new look and new shows begin, and the transitional period is complete

new_dialogue.text: Did you see that old marble statue they pulled from the sea near Sicily? The one covered in barnacles.

Yeah—finds like that are wild. Saltwater can preserve details surprisingly well, but the cleanup is delicate so they don’t strip the surface. Do you mean the bronze-looking figure they think might be from a shipwreck?
That’s the one. I always wonder how they even locate wrecks without just sweeping the whole ocean.
Usually a mix of sonar mapping, magnetometers (for metal), ROV cameras, and a lot of targeted searching from historical records. It’s less “sweep everything” and more “narrow down likely spots.”
Makes sense. On a totally different note—do you think a story needs a villain to be interesting?

Not necessarily. Conflict can come from nature, time pressure, misunderstandings, internal doubts, or competing goals between otherwise decent people. A “villain” is just one common shape conflict can take.
Give me an example of a plot without a villain but still with tension.

A rescue mission during a sudden storm: the tension comes from limited oxygen, shifting terrain, and tough decisions about who goes where—not from anyone being evil. Or a team racing to fix a failing satellite before it re-enters, where the antagonist is physics and the clock.
That co-host tease makes way more sense now—Nestor’s the only “prince” they’ve been quietly dusting off in the bumpers since the WOQ comeback started getting hinted.

Yep, and it lines up with the way they’ve been reintroducing WOQ without making it feel like a random add-on. Having Prince Nestor pop up in the bumpers first is basically a soft-launch, then pairing him with Gloopy for the Fall preview is the full “welcome back.”
